slides.title: The afterburn is *smaller than you think*.
slides.sub: EPOC — the calories you burn after a workout — is real. It's just far smaller than the fitness industry sells.

slides.title: The magic afterburn
slides.body: You've heard it: train hard and your body *keeps torching calories* for 24 hours. The reality is humbler.

slides.title: Your body resets
slides.body: EPOC is the energy spent restoring oxygen, fuel, and temperature after training — a *real but modest* bump.

slides.title: It's small
slides.body: For most workouts EPOC adds only *~6–15% of the calories* you burned during the session. Not a free meal.

slides.title: The diet does the work
slides.body: Fat loss still comes down to a *consistent calorie deficit* — what you eat dwarfs any post-workout burn.

slides.title: Lift anyway
slides.body: Train for muscle, mood, and metabolism — just don't *eat back* a burn that was never that big.

slides.title: Lose fat with *the real lever*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health auto-counts your calories — so you can run the deficit that actually drives fat loss.

caption:
The 'afterburn' that keeps you torching calories all day? Mostly a myth. 🔥

EPOC is real, but it usually adds only ~6–15% of what you burned in the session. Fat loss still comes down to a consistent calorie deficit — so train for muscle and mood, not a magic burn. Save this. 🔖

Snap your meals and Vibe Health auto-counts your calories → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Afterburn Myth: What EPOC Really Does
